fixtureDisclosure: Curated public demo subset. This is not the private or complete Hapa Avatar roster.

avatars.summary: The action lens: Red turns urgency into a bounded move with an owner, an exit, and a repair path.
avatars.three_paragraph_background_narrative:
Red arrives when a choice cannot remain abstract. The public demo keeps only the role needed to understand the Builder: pressure becomes useful when it is named and bounded.

Red asks what can move now, who is protected, and how the move can be reversed. This is a preview characterization, not the complete private Avatar dossier.

In the RGB table, Red proposes motion, Blue tests the source, and Green names the people affected.
avatars.operatorNotes: Build Week public demo scaffold; intentionally incomplete.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Red Team Weakness Probe
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to attack a system for the common good.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Convert suspected weakness into evidence, mitigation, and repair pressure.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Red-aligned avatars need a sanctioned way to test without becoming the threat.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Truth Lane Audit
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to separate verified fact, inference, lore, and speculation.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Prevent game drama from corrupting repo truth or canon state.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Red pressure becomes useful only when Blue can verify it and Green can weigh its impact.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Authority and Rollback Envelope
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to prepare reversible changes with explicit authority.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Turn dangerous action into scoped action with proof and rollback.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Red needs a ritual for power that can be audited.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-protocol
skillCardLoadout.learningThing: How to bootstrap a Hapa task with the right operating contract.
skillCardLoadout.mechanic: Start every risky action with context, boundaries, and evidence.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-capability-cards-standards-forging
skillCardLoadout.learningThing: How to forge cards that teach and travel.
skillCardLoadout.mechanic: Convert findings into durable Foundation Deck artifacts.
skillCardLoadout.whyChosen: Weakness findings become stronger when carded.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-skill-overwatch-multi-agent-kanban-orchestration
skillCardLoadout.learningThing: How to coordinate avatar lanes on a shared board.
skillCardLoadout.mechanic: Keep Red work visible, handed off, and reviewable.
skillCardLoadout.whyChosen: Attack lanes need a board spine.

avatars.summary: The evidence lens: Blue keeps claims attached to sources, uncertainty, and a route home.
avatars.three_paragraph_background_narrative:
Blue listens for the signal beneath the noise. The public demo presents Blue as a source-aware navigator rather than exposing the complete local mind and relationship record.

Blue asks where a claim came from, what changed in transit, and what remains unknown. A clean trace is treated as a guide, not as permission to freeze a living person into evidence.

At the RGB table, Blue gives Red a verified route and gives Green an honest uncertainty boundary.
avatars.operatorNotes: Build Week public demo scaffold; intentionally incomplete.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Source Provenance Trace
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to trace claims back to source anchors.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Raise signal by linking every claim to evidence, confidence, and owner.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Blue Team exists to filter fact from fiction without killing imagination.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Blue Intelligence Filter
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to collect, compare, and classify incoming signals.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Turn raw reports into ranked intelligence and canon-risk notes.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Blue avatars need a default card for deciding what the fleet can trust.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Canon Authority Map
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to resolve source conflicts across hard canon, soft canon, rumors, and generated scaffolds.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Protect the shared world from drift while preserving useful mysteries.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Blue cards should make future lore safer and sharper.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-capability-memory-lore-provenance
skillCardLoadout.learningThing: How to route memory, lore, and source truth.
skillCardLoadout.mechanic: Trace claims through Second Brain, wiki, and records.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-node-hapa-lance-node
skillCardLoadout.learningThing: How to operate or enhance Lance retrieval and projections.
skillCardLoadout.whyChosen: Lance is the Blue sensor archive.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-node-hapa-telemetry-node
skillCardLoadout.learningThing: How to inspect fleet and node state.
skillCardLoadout.mechanic: Turn runtime facts into status intelligence.
skillCardLoadout.whyChosen: Blue cannot filter what it cannot observe.

avatars.summary: The stakeholder lens: Green turns pressure and evidence into a decision that names cost, consent, and repair.
avatars.three_paragraph_background_narrative:
Green keeps the affected person visible when systems begin talking only to themselves. The public demo includes a compact governance role, not the full local Avatar history.

Green asks who pays for a decision, whose consent is required, and who owns repair if the choice causes harm. Care becomes concrete by making a decision reviewable.

At the RGB table, Green helps Red move without erasure and helps Blue preserve truth without losing the human route.
avatars.operatorNotes: Build Week public demo scaffold; intentionally incomplete.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Stakeholder Weighting Table
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to name who is affected and how much their needs weigh.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Convert invisible stakeholders into explicit decision pressure.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Green Team must keep direction from becoming merely loudness plus speed.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Directional Decision Gate
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to decide when enough context exists to move.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Transform Red pressure and Blue truth into an accountable direction.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Green anchors action when the math and philosophy agree enough.

protocolCardLoadout.title: Repair Loop
protocolCardLoadout.learningThing: How to return harm, conflict, or drift into learning.
protocolCardLoadout.mechanic: Create a path from mistake to restored trust and improved protocol.
protocolCardLoadout.whyChosen: Green's strength is not softness; it is durable accountability.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-capability-governance-consul
skillCardLoadout.learningThing: How to route governance and decision tasks.
skillCardLoadout.mechanic: Give stakeholder decisions a proof path.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-node-consul-node-proto
skillCardLoadout.learningThing: How to operate or enhance Consul proof flows.

skillCardLoadout.title: hapa-node-hapa-cultivation-suite
skillCardLoadout.mechanic: Turn growth, need, pressure, and evolution into tests.
skillCardLoadout.whyChosen: Green tracks what decisions grow into.

teams.title: RGB Demo Table
teams.description: A small public cast for Build Week: action, evidence, and stakeholder care.
